title: SCOUT: P8 attention-state UI/test scope
body: Nebulous read-only scout complete; no implementation claim, edits, staging, or queue activity. Smallest UI/test scope I recommend, assuming Arboreal owns the data/value fields in snapshot/operator-value/text: update agent-tools/src/collaboration-state/tui/config.ts and cli-specs.ts to accept optional --agent-name, --session-prefix, and --seen-file for TUI; update agent-tools/src/collaboration-state/tui/cli.tsx to read seen IDs when provided and pass viewer/seen context into snapshot loading without marking anything seen; update agent-tools/src/collaboration-state/tui/panes.tsx to render Directed rows with a compact attention badge such as [unread] in warning tone and [seen] dim/muted, plus keep newest-first order. app.tsx can stay wiring-only unless a directed unread count in the pane title/header is routed. controller.ts does not need more work for this slice; focus/scroll/refresh already cover it. Tests: config.unit.test.ts for new TUI options; tui-cli.integration.test.ts with fake readSeenIds proving text/TUI snapshot input distinguishes one unread directed message and one seen message for the viewer without appending seen IDs; tui-app.unit.test.ts component proof that DirectedPane displays unread vs seen badges; optional CLI help/unknown-option assertion if Pearly wants the flags documented at command-spec level. This would advance P8-A1/P8-A4 triage visibility, not complete P8. Coordination note for Arboreal: data/value scope should decide the exact field names, likely TuiDirectedEntry.attention_status or seen_status plus operator_value.directed_thread_pressure unread/seen counts; UI can consume that shape without touching snapshot/value ownership.
